ponta-da-oliveira.jpg.title: File:Cristo Rei - Garajau - Ilha da Madeira - Portugal (51433763852).jpg
ponta-da-oliveira.jpg.page: Cristo Rei - Garajau - Ilha da Madeira - Portugal (51433763852)
ponta-da-oliveira.jpg.artist: Vitor Oliveira from Torres Vedras, PORTUGAL
ponta-da-oliveira.jpg.description: Cristo Rei da Ponta do Garajau, ou Cristo Rei da Madeira, é o monumento ao Sagrado Coração de Jesus erigido na Ponta do Garajau, na ilha da Madeira. Descrição O monumento, com 14 metros de altura, consiste numa estátua de braços abertos voltada para o oceano. Foi mandado construir e pago pelo conselheiro Aires de Ornelas, filho do último morgado do Caniço. A estátua foi executada pelos escultores franceses Pierre Lenoir e Georges Serraz e inaugurada no dia 30 de outubro de 1927. É o mais antigo monumento a Cristo Rei edificado. pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cristo_Rei_da_Ponta_do_Garajau

miradouro-do-guindaste.jpg.title: File:Talefe Faial 573.jpg
miradouro-do-guindaste.jpg.description: Triangulation pillar on the Miradouro do Guindaste near Faial, Madeira, looking west. The pillar is about 1.3 metres (4.3 ft) high. A threaded rod about 50 millimetres (2.0 in) long sticks out from the top for securing the surveying instrument. (The Carta Militar 1:25000 map marks trig posts clearly but it does not mark this one. I assume that this is a trig pillar which has been retired from official use.)

fanalwald.jpg.title: File:Fanal (Madeira, Portugal), Lorbeerwald -- 2025 -- 1527.jpg
fanalwald.jpg.description: Laurel trees (Ocotea foetens) in the laurel forest “Fanal”, Madeira, PortugalFanal is a mystical high plateau area in the northwest of the island of Madeira. It is located in the Laurisilva Forest, an evergreen laurel forest that has been a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1999.Fanal is particularly well known for its centuries-old laurel trees with gnarled, moss-covered branches. The frequent fog creates a fairytale-like, almost surreal atmosphere. The trees are part of an ancient ecosystem that covered large parts of southern Europe millions of years ago.Historically, the area was used for agriculture at times, for example as pasture land. Today, Fanal is a nature reserve and a popular destination for hikers and nature photographers. It is considered one of Madeira’s most impressive natural monuments and symbolizes the island’s original, wild landscape.
